tasks.title: Build text document generation system during world creation
tasks.description: GameTextTypes.ts defines 6 text categories (book, journal, letter, flyer, recipe, notice) with CEFR levels and multi-page support, but no system generates these documents during world creation. Build one. (1) Create server/services/text-document-generator.ts that generates world-specific text documents using the AI provider. (2) For each settlement in the world, generate: 2-3 notice board postings (community events, missing items, job postings), 1-2 letters (from one NPC to another, referencing their relationship), 1-2 recipe cards (local cuisine with ingredients from the item list), 1 journal entry (from the missing writer, containing a clue). (3) Each document should be generated in the target language with: title, body text, translation, difficulty rating (matched to CEFR A1-B2), vocabulary words with definitions, and an optional comprehension question. (4) Documents related to the main quest should have the clueText field populated with relevant mystery clues. (5) Store generated documents in a new 'documents' collection in MongoDB. (6) Add documents to the WorldIR export so they're available in standalone games. (7) Each document needs a spawn location (building ID or lot ID) for placement in the 3D world.

tasks.title: Add Documents editor section to the Insimul web application
tasks.description: World creators need to view, edit, and manage text documents. (1) Add a 'Documents' section to the world editor Data tab. (2) Display all documents grouped by type (books, journals, letters, flyers, recipes, notices) with counts per category. (3) Each document shows: title, type icon, CEFR level badge, word count, associated location, and whether it contains a main quest clue. (4) Click to expand shows: full text (target language), translation, vocabulary words, comprehension question if any. (5) Add 'Generate Documents' button that runs the text document generator for the world. (6) Allow manual editing of any generated document. (7) Allow adding custom documents. (8) Add 'Assign Location' picker that lets creators place documents in specific buildings/lots. (9) API endpoints: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/worlds/:worldId/documents. (10) Typecheck passes.

tasks.title: Place text documents in the 3D game world
tasks.description: Generated documents need to appear as interactable objects in the game world. (1) During world spawning in BabylonGame, load the documents data and create visible props for each: books on shelves/tables inside buildings, letters on desks, flyers on walls near notice boards, recipe cards in restaurant kitchens, journals in the writer's home or hidden locations. (2) Each document prop should have a subtle glow or particle effect to indicate it's interactable (similar to quest items). (3) When the player interacts with a document (press E), open the document reading panel. (4) Documents found for the first time emit a text_collected event. (5) Ensure documents are placed at their assigned spawn locations — if the location is a building interior, the document appears when the player enters that building. (6) For documents without assigned locations, place them semi-randomly: notices near notice boards, letters in residences, books in shops/schools. (7) Track which documents have been collected in the save state.

tasks.title: Build document reading panel with comprehension quiz
tasks.description: When the player reads a text document, show a dedicated reading panel with language learning features. (1) Create shared/game-engine/rendering/DocumentReadingPanel.ts — a Babylon.js GUI panel that displays the document text. (2) Show the document title, type icon, and CEFR level. (3) Display the text in the target language with hover-to-translate on individual words (reuse HoverTranslationSystem pattern). (4) For multi-page documents, add page navigation (Previous/Next). (5) Show a 'Translation' toggle button that reveals the full English translation below the target language text. (6) After reading all pages, if the document has a comprehension question, show the quiz: question text + 4 multiple-choice options. (7) Correct answers award XP (scaled by CEFR level: A1=10, A2=15, B1=25, B2=40) and emit questions_answered event. (8) If the document has a clueText field, display a 'Clue Discovered!' notification and add it to the ClueStore. (9) Add new vocabulary from the document to the player's vocabulary list. (10) Mark the document as 'read' in the game state.

tasks.title: Connect documents to the main quest and notice board
tasks.description: Documents should serve both as language learning content and as narrative devices. (1) The missing writer's journal entries should be key documents that advance the main quest — finding and reading each one should complete a 'collect_text' objective and reveal a clue. (2) Generate 6+ journal entries (one per chapter) that progressively reveal the mystery. (3) Letters between NPCs should provide optional clues and context about the world's social dynamics. (4) Notice board postings should be visible on the settlement notice board (BabylonNoticeBoardPanel already exists) — connect the generated notices to the notice board display. (5) Recipes found in the world should unlock cooking activities at kitchen hotspots. (6) Books found in shops/libraries should teach vocabulary categories (animals, food, professions, etc.) and unlock vocabulary category achievements. (7) Create find_text and read_text quest objectives that reference specific generated documents by ID. (8) The Game Menu's 'Texts' collection (if it exists) should show all found documents organized by type.
